You are optimizing a skill description for a Claude Code skill called "ipak-dev". A "skill" is sort of like a prompt, but with progressive disclosure -- there's a title and description that Claude sees when deciding whether to use the skill, and then if it does use the skill, it reads the .md file which has lots more details and potentially links to other resources in the skill folder like helper files and scripts and additional documentation or examples.

The description appears in Claude's "available_skills" list. When a user sends a query, Claude decides whether to invoke the skill based solely on the title and on this description. Your goal is to write a description that triggers for relevant queries, and doesn't trigger for irrelevant ones.

Skill content (for context on what the skill does):
<skill_content>
---
name: ipak-dev
description: >
  Use this skill when working on ipak.dev — the personal brand website for Sergei Pak (AI Systems Architect & CEO). Triggers on: frontend UI work, component builds, page design, visual redesign, landing page, hero sections, animations, typography, color palette, CSS/Tailwind styling, layout changes. Also triggers for design system decisions, motion design, and visual direction for ipak.dev project files.
---

# ipak-dev Frontend Design Skill

Personal brand site for Sergei Pak — AI Systems Architect & CEO.
Kazakhstan/CIS market. Premium positioning.

---

## AESTHETIC DIRECTION

Commit to luxury/refined aesthetic **before writing any code**.

### Typography
- Display: distinctive editorial font (e.g. Playfair Display, Cormorant Garamond, Editorial New)
- Body: refined humanist (e.g. Lora, DM Sans at 300–400 weight, Neue Haas Grotesk)
- **NEVER use**: Inter, Roboto, Arial, Space Grotesk, Helvetica Neue as primary
- Scale: large contrast between display (clamp 4rem–8rem) and body (16–18px)

### Color System
- Canvas: `#0e1011` (dark OLED, near-black with warm undertone)
- Surface: `#141618` / `#1a1d20`
- Borders: `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` – `rgba(255,255,255,0.10)`
- Text primary: `#f0ede8` (warm off-white)
- Text secondary: `rgba(240,237,232,0.55)`
- Accent: single precise color — e.g. `#c8a96e` (gold) or `#8b7355` (bronze)
- **NO** purple gradients. **NO** blue-purple UI-kit palettes. Depth through layered transparencies, NOT brightness.

### Backgrounds & Texture
- Grain overlay: `url(noise.svg)` at 3–6% opacity over every section
- Layered transparencies: `backdrop-filter: blur(12px)` for glass cards
- Dramatic shadows: `box-shadow: 0 40px 100px rgba(0,0,0,0.6)`
- Subtle radial glow behind hero: `radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 40% at 50% 0%, rgba(200,169,110,0.08), transparent)`

### Layout Principles
- Asymmetry: never center-stack everything; break grid deliberately
- Overlap: elements bleed across grid boundaries
- Generous negative space: section padding `clamp(6rem, 12vw, 14rem)`
- Grid-breaking: hero text over 60vw, image clips outside container
- **NEVER**: 3-column icon feature grids, cookie-cutter card rows

---

## MOTION SYSTEM

### Page Load Sequence (staggered)
```css
@keyframes fadeUp {
  from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(24px); }
  to   { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
}
.animate-in { animation: fadeUp 0.7s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1) both; }
.delay-1 { animation-delay: 0.1s; }
.delay-2 { animation-delay: 0.2s; }
/* etc. */
```

### Spring Hover (JS)
```js
// Magnetic button effect
el.addEventListener('mousemove', e => {
  const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
  const x = (e.clientX - rect.left - rect.width/2) * 0.3;
  const y = (e.clientY - rect.top  - rect.height/2) * 0.3;
  el.style.transform = `translate(${x}px, ${y}px)`;
});
el.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => {
  el.style.transform = '';
  el.style.transition = 'transform 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1)';
});
```

### Scroll Parallax
- Hero subtitle: `translateY` at 0.15× scroll speed
- Background grain: `translateY` at 0.05× (barely perceptible depth)

---

## NEVER PRODUCE

| Forbidden Pattern | Reason |
|---|---|
| Purple gradients on white | UI-kit cliché |
| 3-column icon feature grids | Generic SaaS template |
| Gradient-fill buttons | Cheap look |
| Emoji in headings | Unprofessional |
| `bg-gradient-to-r from-purple-500 to-pink-500` | Absolutely not |
| Centered hero with stock photo background | Too generic |
| Shadcn/Radix default styling without customization | Wrong aesthetic |

---

## DESIGN PARAMETERS

```
DESIGN_VARIANCE   = 7   (push unusual composition choices)
MOTION_INTENSITY  = 5   (purposeful, not distracting)
VISUAL_DENSITY    = 2   (breathe — minimal elements per screen)
```

---

## TECH STACK (ipak.dev)

- Next.js 14 / React 18 (or static HTML+CSS for landing)
- Tailwind CSS with custom config extending the palette above
- Framer Motion for spring animations
- CSS custom properties for all tokens
- No heavy UI libs (no MUI, no Chakra, no default Shadcn themes)

## OUTPUT RULES

1. Always output **complete files** — no truncation, no `// ... rest unchanged`
2. Every component: add `animate-in` + staggered `delay-N` classes
3. Every interactive element: magnetic hover or spring transform
4. First thing in any session: propose 2–3 aesthetic directions before coding

</skill_content>

Based on the failures, write a new and improved description that is more likely to trigger correctly. When I say "based on the failures", it's a bit of a tricky line to walk because we don't want to overfit to the specific cases you're seeing. So what I DON'T want you to do is produce an ever-expanding list of specific queries that this skill should or shouldn't trigger for. Instead, try to generalize from the failures to broader categories of user intent and situations where this skill would be useful or not useful. The reason for this is twofold:

1. Avoid overfitting
2. The list might get loooong and it's injected into ALL queries and there might be a lot of skills, so we don't want to blow too much space on any given description.

Concretely, your description should not be more than about 100-200 words, even if that comes at the cost of accuracy. There is a hard limit of 1024 characters — descriptions over that will be truncated, so stay comfortably under it.

Here are some tips that we've found to work well in writing these descriptions:
- The skill should be phrased in the imperative -- "Use this skill for" rather than "this skill does"
- The skill description should focus on the user's intent, what they are trying to achieve, vs. the implementation details of how the skill works.
- The description competes with other skills for Claude's attention — make it distinctive and immediately recognizable.
- If you're getting lots of failures after repeated attempts, change things up. Try different sentence structures or wordings.

I'd encourage you to be creative and mix up the style in different iterations since you'll have multiple opportunities to try different approaches and we'll just grab the highest-scoring one at the end. 

Please respond with only the new description text in <new_description> tags, nothing else.

Use this skill for ipak.dev frontend design: page layouts, hero sections, components, color systems, typography, animations, CSS styling, and visual refinement. Implements dark luxury aesthetic with editorial fonts, sophisticated motion, and premium brand positioning.
</new_description>
